title: Arm moves beyond IP with AGI CPU silicon 136-core data center chip targets AI infrastructure with Meta as lead partner
summary: Arm has developed an in-house, up-to 136-core CPU for data centers, targeting AI infrastructure with Meta as a lead partner.
insight: Arm expands into AI-focused hardware, increasing competition for traditional chipmakers.

title: Microsoft and Nvidia launch AI partnership to speed up nuclear power plant permitting and construction simulation tools and generative models could hasten historically lengthy processes
summary: Microsoft and Nvidia partner to speed up nuclear power plant construction with AI, using generative AI, digital twin simulation, and Nvidia's Omniverse platform to streamline the nuclear lifecycle.
insight: Accelerating nuclear power plant construction for AI data centers' growing demand.

title: AYANEO's upcoming Next 2 handheld gaming console shelved due to rising component prices company stops preorders for the $1,999 Strix Halo device
summary: Ayaneo has suspended sales of its premium gaming handheld due to rising component costs. Existing preorders will still be honored. The company cited unfeasible procurement costs, particularly for storage, as the reason behind the decision.
insight: Rising component prices threaten profitability of high-end gaming devices.

title: SK hynix places record $8 billion order for ASML EUV lithography machines should pay for up to 30 EUV machines over two years, serving HBM and advanced DRAM production
summary: SK hynix placed a record order for 11.9 trillion won ($8 billion) worth of EUV lithography equipment from ASML. This equipment will be used to support the production of HBM and advanced DRAM memory. The deal will span two years.
insight: Massive investment in EUV lithography drives semiconductor technology advancement.

title: We need your opinions to shape Tom's Hardware Premium
summary: Tom's Hardware Premium is seeking feedback from non-subscribers to shape its platform. Participants can win a gift card for their input. Share your opinions to help improve the service.
insight: Subscriber feedback shapes Tom's Hardware Premium's future development direction

title: Huawei unveils new Atlas 350 AI accelerator with 1.56 PFLOPS of FP4 compute and up to 112GB of HBM claims 2.8x more performance than Nvidia's H20
summary: Huawei unveiled the Atlas 350 AI accelerator with 1.56 PFLOPS FP4 compute and up to 112GB HBM. It claims 2.8x more performance than Nvidia's H20. The Atlas 350 uses the Ascend 950PR chip.
insight: Huawei's Atlas 350 AI accelerator surpasses Nvidia's performance claims.

title: US senators want to suspend Nvidia AI chip export licenses to China and its intermediaries bipartisan letter to Commerce Dept says that Huang s claims of no chip diversion were contradicted by reporting available
summary: US senators Warren and Banks urge Commerce Dept to suspend Nvidia export licenses to China due to concerns over AI chip diversion.
insight: US-China chip export controls have significant implications for global tech trade.

title: Pentagon formalizes Palantir's Maven AI as a core military system with multi-year funding platform's investment grows to $13 billion from $480 million in 2024
summary: The Pentagon designates Palantir's Maven Smart System as an official program, securing multi-year funding.
insight: Pentagon commitment cements AI's crucial role in military targeting operations.

title: Intel Core Ultra 5 250K Plus review: The new best $200 CPU
summary: Intel's Core Ultra 5 250K Plus offers exceptional performance at an affordable price, setting a new standard for $200 CPUs. It matches or surpasses competitors in various benchmarks, delivering impressive results in games and applications. This makes it an excellent choice for budget-conscious builders and gamers.
insight: Unbeatable value for budget-friendly CPU performance in $200 category

title: Microsoft-backed start-up raises $40 million for helium atom beam lithography that could print chips at atomic resolution 0.1nm beam is 135 times narrower than ASML's EUV light
summary: Lace Lithography secured $40 million in funding for a helium atom beam lithography tool that could print chips at atomic resolution.
insight: Atomic-resolution chipmaking could boost computing power and memory density significantly.

title: Autonomous mobile drone swarm killer created in trilateral US defense company collab the new Leonidas Autonomous Ground Vehicle (AGV) is a high-power microwave platform
summary: A US defense company collaboration has developed the Leonidas AGV, a high-power microwave platform designed to counter drone swarms. This autonomous ground vehicle is a significant response to emerging threats. It leverages directed energy capabilities for effective countermeasures.
insight: Directed energy counters swarms with high-power microwave precision.

title: China laptop vendor eats humble pie, apology says production error was behind chip mix-up full refunds offered to those affected by the fake Ryzen 5 7430U scam
summary: Chuwi apologizes for laptops with mismatched specs, offers full refunds
insight: Chinese laptop vendor's reputation hinges on addressing spec discrepancies

title: AWS Bahrain suffers major disruption due to the ongoing US-Iran conflict drone activity blamed for service interruption
summary: AWS Bahrain experienced a major disruption due to the US-Iran conflict, with drone activity cited as the cause. The company has requested clients to migrate their workloads to other regions. This has led to service interruptions.
insight: Global conflicts can impact cloud infrastructure and business operations.

title: Gigabyte X870 Aorus Stealth Motherboard Review: The gothy Goldilocks of rear-connect AM5 motherboards
summary: Gigabyte's X870 Aorus Stealth offers a high-performance AM5 motherboard at a budget-friendly price.
insight: Reasonably priced BTF options fill AM5 motherboard market gap.

title: FCC bans import of new consumer routers not made in the US over security threat agency says foreign-made devices pose unacceptable risk to US persons
summary: The FCC bans import of new foreign-made consumer routers due to security threats. Foreign-made devices are deemed an unacceptable risk to US citizens. Manufacturers must secure 'Conditional Approval' from the DoD or DHS.
insight: Foreign-made routers pose significant security risks to US citizens' data.

title: Trump administration targets $4 trillion Pax Silica investment fund for semiconductors the US will start with a $250 million investment for global consortium
summary: The Trump administration targets $4 trillion Pax Silica for semiconductor investment.
insight: $4 trillion investment aims to boost US semiconductor industry competitiveness.

title: Microsoft says, for once, Windows update isn't to blame for Galaxy Book4 laptops losing access to C: drive the Galaxy Connect app was the culprit, and it's been taken down from Microsoft Store
summary: A widely reported issue with Galaxy Book 4 devices was caused by the Galaxy Connect app, not Windows Update. The app has been removed from the Microsoft Store. Microsoft has provided recovery steps for those affected.
insight: Correct app identification prevents unnecessary Windows Update blame

title: Thermal pads with in-built vapor-chambers claim 50 to 80 times better thermal conductivity than normal thermal pads 1,200 W/m-K "Vapor-Pad" from Xerendipity designed to replace traditional TIM in a CPU
summary: Xerendipity's Vapor-Pad offers improved thermal conductivity for phone SoCs, enhancing cooling without added cost or thickness.
insight: Enhanced phone performance through improved heat dissipation and reduced throttling.

title: Price of Nvidia's Vera Rubin NVL72 racks skyrockets to as much as $8.8 million apiece, but server makers' margins will be tight Nvidia is moving closer to shipping entire full-scale systems
summary: Nvidia's Vera Rubin NVL72 racks now cost up to $8.8 million each. Server manufacturers may face reduced profit margins due to increased costs. Nvidia is moving towards shipping complete full-scale systems.
insight: High-priced Nvidia hardware tightens server manufacturers' profit margins significantly

title: RainbowLink-V2 compact USB-to-serial converter features 12V output, dual TTL, isolated RS-485 and RS-232 interfaces
summary: DFRobot's RainbowLink-V2 is a compact USB-to-serial converter featuring four independent channels and multiple output voltages. It provides isolated RS-485 and RS-232 interfaces, dual TTL interfaces, and a 12V output. The device eliminates the need for a separate power adapter.
insight: Simplified serial communication setup with integrated power supply capabilities.

title: LooperRobotics Insight 9 standalone spatial AI camera features D-Robotics RDK X5 SoC, supports ROS 2 (Crowdfunding)
summary: LooperRobotics Insight 9 is a standalone spatial AI camera for embodied intelligence, integrating a D-Robotics RDK X5 SoC for on-device processing and featuring a Tri-Eye Perception Matrix with a wide field of view and automotive-grade IMU.
insight: Autonomous spatial AI camera with on-device processing for robotics applications.

title: Upcoming ESP32-S31 dual-core RISC-V MCU offers Gigabit Ethernet, WiFi, Bluetooth, and 802.15.4 connectivity
summary: Espressif Systems' ESP32-S31 is a dual-core RISC-V MCU with Gigabit Ethernet, WiFi, and Bluetooth connectivity, featuring a high-performance core with FPU and SIMD instructions, a low-power core, and various peripherals.
insight: High-performance core enables efficient processing for IoT and industrial applications.

title: Jury reaches verdict in social media addiction trial, finds Meta liable for harm to children
summary: A New Mexico jury finds Meta liable for harming children through social media, citing platform safety concerns and public deception.
insight: Social media giants held accountable for child harm and safety.

title: Arm just changed the rules, building its first-ever CPU and betting big on agentic AI
summary: Arm is entering the CPU market with its first-ever CPU, disrupting data center dominance. This strategic move shifts the focus from GPUs and accelerators. Arm's agentic AI bet signals significant innovation.
insight: GPUs' three-year data center reign is being challenged by Arm's CPU entry.

title: "Silicon" is a new coffee table book about the chip industry's origins and impact
summary: The book 'Silicon' explores the chip industry's origins and impact through 10 chapters, each focusing on a different aspect of how silicon transformed modern life. The narrative delves into the process of turning silicon into machine intelligence, highlighting its significance in shaping our world.
insight: Silicon's evolution revolutionized technology, transforming human life forever

title: Epic cuts 1,000+ jobs amid financial struggles, seeks half-billion-dollar cost savings
summary: Epic Games cuts over 1,000 jobs in an effort to reduce costs. The company seeks to achieve a cost savings of half a billion dollars. This move is attributed to financial struggles and industry-wide changes.
insight: Industry shifts and financial struggles drive major layoffs at Epic Games.

title: This startup will pay you $800 to yell at AI all day
summary: Memvid is offering $800 for a 'professional' yeller to stress-test popular chatbots, mimicking the behavior of Boston Dynamics' AI testers.
insight: AI robustness tested through human interaction and stress scenarios.

title: Microsoft signals potential end to Windows 11 online account requirement
summary: Microsoft's VP, Scott Hanselman, expressed dislike for Windows 11's online requirement, hinting at possible changes.
insight: Microsoft may reconsider Windows 11's online account requirement policy.

title: The US moves to block most new routers made overseas
summary: The US blocks most new routers made overseas, affecting future imports.
insight: US router import ban impacts foreign tech industry significantly.

title: There were 1,000 Internet devices in 1984, 1 million in 1992, by 2025 that figure reached...
summary: The number of internet devices grew from 1,000 in 1984 to 1 million in 1992, and by 2025, it reached 50 billion. This rapid increase is attributed to advancements in technology and the widespread adoption of the internet. The sheer scale of connected devices is a testament to human innovation.
insight: Unprecedented digital transformation drives exponential growth of connected devices.

title: Broadcom sounds the alarm as chip demand is pushing TSMC to its limits
summary: Broadcom's Natarajan Ramachandran warns that TSMC is hitting capacity limits. This is a concern for Broadcom as it relies on TSMC for chip production. TSMC's capacity expansion plans are extending through 2027.
insight: Chip demand is outpacing TSMC's manufacturing capacity expansion.

title: PowerVR GPUs gain DirectX support as Imagination eyes PC market comeback
summary: Imagination Technologies adds DirectX support to PowerVR GPUs, aiming for PC comeback.
insight: Imagination expands its reach beyond mobile markets to PC gaming sector.

title: ESRB won't adopt Europe's stricter loot box rating changes in the US
summary: The ESRB won't adopt stricter loot box rating changes from Europe. Germany's ratings board implemented higher ratings for games with microtransactions in 2023, but the ESRB has no plans to follow suit. This decision leaves the US with less regulatory oversight on loot box content.
insight: Lack of US oversight on loot box content sparks gamer concerns.

title: Crimson Desert dev apologizes for AI art slop, promises to add Intel Arc GPU support
summary: Pearl Abyss apologizes for AI-generated placeholder art in Crimson Desert, promises optimization for Intel Arc GPUs.
insight: Developer accountability for AI-generated content in games is crucial for trust.

title: Steve Wozniak says he's "disappointed a lot" by AI and rarely uses it
summary: Steve Wozniak expresses disappointment and limited use of AI technology.
insight: Tech pioneer's skepticism highlights AI concerns despite industry advancements.

title: High school student develops membrane-free filter that removes most microplastics from water
summary: High school student develops a membrane-free filter to remove most microplastics from water, addressing contamination issues in local drinking water.
insight: Effective water treatment can mitigate health risks from microplastic contamination.

title: Intel Core Ultra 5 250K Plus Review: They Did It
summary: Intel's Core Ultra 5 250K Plus offers impressive productivity and gaming performance at an affordable price of $200.
insight: Intel corrects 200 series flaws with a budget-friendly performance boost.
